message:
fix(obligation): detect duplicate obligation reserve in dialectica contract checker

The ContractChecker's Reserve event handler silently overwrote existing
obligations when the same obligation ID was reserved twice. This masked a
real bug: the first reservation's lifecycle (and any pending
ExhaustiveResolution) would be lost, making the NoPartialCommit contract
unenforceable for that obligation.

Now, if an obligation is already present in the tracker when a second Reserve
arrives, we record a NoPartialCommit violation with full diagnostic context
(existing state, original reservation timestamp) and return early without
overwriting the original snapshot.

Adds a targeted test (duplicate_reserve_detected) that feeds two Reserve
events for the same obligation ID and asserts the checker reports a
NoPartialCommit violation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
